mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate elevated above 4.0% signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates on equity valuations and raises corporate borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with emerging labor weakness
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with UNRATE changes, or Spearman rank correlation < -0.10 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Decline in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with contraction in housing starts (HOUST) signals synchronized demand collapse across consumer and construction sectors, triggering negative equity returns as forward earnings estimates are revised downward over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction across consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or joint signal has Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) declines signal supply-side weakness and reduced capacity utilization, indicating economic contraction that depresses equity valuations and forward earnings growth over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with INDPRO declines, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) contraction signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce household income and consumption, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns as consumer discretionary earnings are revised downward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances with PAYEMS declines > 0.3%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) signals monetary policy constraint that limits central bank accommodation and increases real discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations.
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure with constrained monetary policy
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or joint interaction signal has Rank IC < 0.06 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) deceleration below 2% annualized rate signals late-cycle slowdown that reduces forward earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as growth forecasts are revised downward.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with emerging growth deceleration
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with GDPC1 growth rates below 2%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) contraction below 1200K units signals residential construction collapse that reduces forward GDP growth and employment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as cyclical earnings are revised downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of instances when HOUST < 1200K, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
